title: ""
image: "../../images/py_kurs_cover03.webp"
toc: true
toc-depth: 3
page-layout: full
---

<br>

::: {.d-flex .gap-2}
[Home](../../index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[Python Overview](../index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[1](./01basics1.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[2](./02basics2.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[3](./03basics3.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[4](./04basics4.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[5](./05pandas1.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[6](./06pandas2.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[7](./07Visualisations.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[8](./08WrapUp_Refresher.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
[9](./09QA_SampleExam.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-sm}
:::

# Week 3: Python Basics III
*The foundation of your Python journey.*

::: {layout-ncol=1}
### (Test your Understanding)
* **Python Basics I-II**

### (Fundamentals)
* [**Python Tuples**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_tuples.asp){.new-tab} (from week 2)
* [**Python Sets**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_sets.asp){.new-tab} (from week 2)
* [**Python Dictionaries**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_dictionaries.asp){.new-tab} (from week 2)
* [**Python Conditions**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_conditions.asp){.new-tab}
* [**Python While Loops**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_while_loops.asp){.new-tab}
* [**Python For Loops**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_for_loops.asp){.new-tab}

### (Project)
* [**Project 3: Random Number Guesser**](https://stads-python-course.github.io/projects/project-3.html){.new-tab}
* Review [**Project 3**](https://stads-python-course.github.io/projects/project-3.html){.new-tab}

### (Fundamentals)
* [**Python Functions**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_functions.asp){.new-tab}
* [**Python Lambda**](https://www.w3schools.com/python/python_lambda.asp){.new-tab}
:::
<br>

## Project 3: Random Number Guesser

::: {.panel-tabset}

### The Task
Your task is to code the number guesser game. You should generate a random number and the user has to keep guessing until they find the number.

To do:

Initialize a random number.
Create a loop that runs until the number is guessed.
Ask the user for their guess.
If the guessed number equals the actual number then print "Hurraaa you won! GUESSED_NUMBER was the correct guess."
If the guessed number is larger than the actual number then print "Go lower!"
If the guessed number is smaller than the actual number then print "Go higher!"

Optional:

Implement lives so the user must guess the number within a limited number of tries.
Give the user the chance to play again after a correct guess.

### Hint

Here is a description of all steps required:

- Import the random library
- Create a random number between 0 and 100
- Create an infinite loop and ask the user for a guess inside the loop
- Check inside the loop if the guess equals the random number. If so print "Hurraaa you won! {guess} was the correct guess."
    - At this point you can optionally ask the user if they want to play again. If they answer yes then overwrite the random number with a new random number
    - If they don't want to play again or you don't want to implement the option to play again: break the loop
- Else if the guess is larger than the random number print "Go lower!"
- Else print "Go higher!"

:::

## Resources

::: {layout-ncol=2}
[GitHub Repo](https://www.github.com/STADS-Mannheim/python-course-2025/tree/main){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg .w-100 .new-tab}

[w3schools Tutorial](https://www.w3schools.com/python/){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-lg .w-100 .new-tab}
:::
